title: Why Hospitality in India is Buzzing (And What WeГўв‚¬в„ўre Hearing on the Ground)

excerpt: IndiaГўв‚¬в„ўs hospitality scene is shifting fast, and weГўв‚¬в„ўre here for it. From guest expectations to digital check-ins, hereГўв‚¬в„ўs what weГўв‚¬в„ўre learning directly from the leaders making it happen
body:
IndiaГўв‚¬в„ўs hospitality scene is buzzing.

If youГўв‚¬в„ўve been to Mumbai or Bengaluru recently, youГўв‚¬в„ўll know what we mean. Hotels are packed, guests expect everything instantly, and the entire industry is in the middle of a digital shift that feels inevitableГўв‚¬вЂќand exciting.

In recent conversations with @Shiva from IHCL, weГўв‚¬в„ўre seeing the growing need for balancing scale with guest intimacy in IndiaГўв‚¬в„ўs luxury hospitality sector. The demand for instant, seamless service is here, and hotels are looking to technology not as a replacement for warmth, but as a tool to keep up with guests who expect speed without losing the personal touch. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs clear this innovation is new, and weГўв‚¬в„ўre excited that DialZero is leading this shift to help hotels make that balance possible.

WeГўв‚¬в„ўre grateful to be in operations with forward-thinking leaders like Shiva and the teams at IHCL who are pushing the industry forward while staying true to what makes hospitality meaningful.

ItГўв‚¬в„ўs not just about digital. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs about speed.

A recent McKinsey report found that 75% of consumers expect assistance within five minutes of making contact onlineГўв‚¬вЂќand itГўв‚¬в„ўs not hard to see how this expectation carries into their hotel stay, where waiting for a towel or WiFi instructions feels like an eternity. Hotels in India are recognizing this, and the pressure to deliver Гўв‚¬Е“right nowГўв‚¬Вќ service while maintaining quality is real.

For us at DialZero, itГўв‚¬в„ўs energizing to see hospitality in India embracing these changes. This shift isnГўв‚¬в„ўt about replacing warm service with cold automation. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs about using the right tools to give guests what they want faster while freeing staff to focus on human connectionГўв‚¬вЂќthe very thing that makes hospitality feel like home.

WeГўв‚¬в„ўre grateful to be part of this journey, learning alongside leaders who are pushing the industry forward while keeping its heart intact.

And if your hotel team is feeling the pressure to Гўв‚¬Е“move as fast as your guests,Гўв‚¬Вќ weГўв‚¬в„ўd love to chat (over chai, obviously) about how you can keep pace while keeping your teamГўв‚¬в„ўs sanity intact.

Because if thereГўв‚¬в„ўs one thing weГўв‚¬в„ўve learned in India, itГўв‚¬в„ўs that when hospitality booms, it really booms. And itГўв‚¬в„ўs better to be ready.
